text: Agile methodology is a popular way to manage projects. Instead of doing all the work at once, teams use iterative development. They break the project into small cycles called sprints, which usually last two to four weeks. Each sprint starts with a planning meeting where the team picks tasks from a backlog—a list of work to do. These tasks are written as user stories, describing a feature from the user's perspective. Every day, the team has a short standup meeting to share progress and discuss any problems. This approach allows teams to adapt quickly to changes and deliver value step by step.
